channel.title: Tool Comparisons on ToolVersus — Honest SaaS & AI Tool Comparisons
channel.description: Recent content in Tool Comparisons on ToolVersus — Honest SaaS & AI Tool Comparisons

item.title: CyberGhost vs NordVPN (2026): Budget or Best-in-Class?
item.description:
The Quick Answer

NordVPN is the better VPN. Faster, better audited, more reliable for streaming, broader feature set.

CyberGhost wins on:

  • Headline price — $2.03/mo on 2-year plans (vs NordVPN’s $3.39/mo)
  • Server count — 11,700+ servers vs NordVPN’s 6,400+
  • Money-back window — 45 days vs NordVPN’s 30

For most people, NordVPN’s premium is worth the $1.36/mo difference — the speed gap, audit frequency, and streaming consistency compound quickly. Sign up for NordVPN →

item.title: ProtonVPN vs Mullvad (2026): Which Privacy-First VPN Should You Choose?
item.description:
The Quick Answer

For most people: ProtonVPN. Bigger network (5,800 vs 700 servers), free plan, better streaming support, and if you also want encrypted email/drive, the Unlimited bundle is genuinely good value.

For privacy purists: Mullvad. It’s the only mainstream VPN where you can sign up with literally zero PII — you get a random 16-digit number, and you can mail cash in an envelope to pay. No other VPN even comes close on this axis.

item.title: ProtonVPN vs NordVPN (2026): Which Premium VPN Should You Pick?
item.description:
The Quick Answer

NordVPN is the better all-rounder. It’s cheaper ($3.39/mo vs $4.99/mo on 2-year plans), faster in almost every independent speed test, has more servers, and unblocks streaming services more reliably.

ProtonVPN wins if:

  • You already use Proton Mail / Drive (the Unlimited bundle is genuinely good value)
  • Open-source apps matter to you (ProtonVPN’s are fully OSS; NordVPN’s aren’t)
  • You want a free plan to test first
  • You’re in a heavily-surveilled situation where Swiss jurisdiction matters

For everyone else, NordVPN. Sign up for NordVPN →

item.title: Surfshark vs Mullvad (2026): Cheap & Feature-Rich vs Pure Privacy?
item.description:
The Quick Answer

These two VPNs barely compete on the same axis:

Surfshark is a mainstream consumer VPN — cheap, feature-packed, works on unlimited devices. Great for a family or a general-purpose privacy + geoblock tool.

Mullvad is a privacy-extremist tool — no accounts, no tracking, accepts cash payment. Fewer features, fewer servers, no streaming. Priced as a flat €5/mo with no deals.

If you're not specifically trying to sign up for a VPN without leaving any PII, Surfshark is almost certainly the better product for you. If you are trying to do that, Mullvad is one of two or three options that will let you (and the other is sketchier).

item.title: ActiveCampaign vs GetResponse (2026): Advanced Automation vs All-in-One with Webinars
item.description:
The Quick Answer

ActiveCampaign if email automation is your primary need — lead scoring, complex workflows, CRM integration, and advanced segmentation. GetResponse if you want an all-in-one platform that includes webinars and sales funnels alongside email marketing.

Pricing

Contacts ActiveCampaign (Starter) GetResponse (Email Marketing)
1,000 $15/mo $19/mo
5,000 $79/mo $59/mo
10,000 $111/mo $79/mo
25,000 $179/mo $174/mo

GetResponse is cheaper at 5K+ contacts. ActiveCampaign is cheaper at entry level. The pricing gap narrows at higher tiers.

item.title: ActiveCampaign vs Mailchimp (2026): Automation Powerhouse vs Popular Default
item.description:
The Quick Answer

ActiveCampaign is for businesses that take email seriously — lead nurturing sequences, sales automation, CRM integration, and sophisticated segmentation. Mailchimp is for businesses that want something quick and simple to send newsletters and basic campaigns.

If you’ll outgrow “send a newsletter every week,” start with ActiveCampaign. You’ll save the migration headache later.

Pricing

Contacts ActiveCampaign (Starter) Mailchimp (Standard)
1,000 $15/mo $30/mo
5,000 $79/mo $75/mo
10,000 $111/mo $110/mo
25,000 $179/mo $270/mo

Surprise: ActiveCampaign is cheaper at most tiers, especially at 1,000 contacts ($15 vs $30) and 25,000+ contacts. Mailchimp’s pricing has increased significantly since the Intuit acquisition.

item.title: Bluehost vs SiteGround (2026): Both WordPress.org Recommended — Which Is Actually Better?
item.description:
The Quick Answer

Both are WordPress.org recommended, but SiteGround is the better host — faster servers, better support, daily backups included, 11 data centers vs 2. Bluehost costs less at renewal ($12.99 vs $17.99) and offers phone support. Choose based on whether you value performance or savings.

Side by Side

Feature Bluehost SiteGround
Intro price $1.99/mo $1.99/mo
Renewal $12.99/mo $17.99/mo
Data centers 2 (US only) 11 (global)
Daily backups Extra cost Free (all plans)
Phone support Yes No
G2 rating 3.9/5 4.4/5
Trustpilot 4.6/5 4.7/5
Server tech Apache NGINX + SuperCacher

SiteGround wins on every technical metric. Bluehost wins on price and phone support.

item.title: ConvertKit vs MailerLite (2026): Creator Tools vs Best Value
item.description:
The Quick Answer

Both are excellent platforms loved by creators and small businesses. MailerLite wins on pure value — more features, lower price, higher G2 rating (4.7 vs 4.4). ConvertKit (Kit) wins if you need built-in digital product sales, the Creator Network for audience growth, or you specifically identify as a content creator.

Pricing

Subscribers ConvertKit (Creator) MailerLite (Growing Business)
Free tier 10,000 subs (limited) 1,000 subs (12K emails/mo)
1,000 $25/mo $15/mo
5,000 $79/mo $39/mo
10,000 $119/mo $73/mo

MailerLite is 40-50% cheaper on paid plans. The free tier comparison is interesting — ConvertKit offers more subscribers (10K vs 1K) but with severe feature limitations (no automation, no sequences). MailerLite’s free plan includes automation.

item.title: Hostinger vs Bluehost (2026): Budget King vs WordPress Default
item.description:
The Quick Answer

Hostinger wins. It’s the fastest-growing host in the world (29M+ users) for good reason — better speeds, lower pricing, more data centers, and higher satisfaction scores. Bluehost rides on its WordPress.org endorsement, but Hostinger’s LiteSpeed servers outperform Bluehost’s Apache setup in every speed test.

Pricing Comparison

Plan tier Hostinger Bluehost
Entry $1.99/mo (Premium, 100 sites) $1.99/mo (Basic, 10 sites)
Mid $2.99/mo (Business, 100 sites) $4.99/mo (Choice Plus, unlimited)
Renewal ~$7.99/mo ~$12.99/mo

Same entry price, but Hostinger gives you 100 websites where Bluehost only gives 10. At renewal, Hostinger is 38% cheaper. Over a 3-year term, Hostinger saves you approximately $180.

item.title: Hostinger vs GoDaddy Hosting (2026): Modern Speed vs Legacy Brand
item.description:
The Quick Answer

Hostinger wins. GoDaddy is a great domain registrar but a mediocre web host. Hostinger is 3x cheaper, 50% faster, and rated significantly higher by users on every review platform. GoDaddy’s only advantages are brand familiarity and 24/7 phone support.

Pricing

Hostinger (Premium) GoDaddy (Economy)
Intro price $1.99/mo $5.99/mo
Renewal ~$7.99/mo ~$11.99/mo
Websites 100 1
Storage 100 GB NVMe 25 GB

Hostinger is 3x cheaper at intro and 33% cheaper at renewal while offering 100 websites and 4x the storage.

item.title: Hostinger vs SiteGround (2026): Budget vs Premium — Is 4x the Price Worth It?
item.description:
The Quick Answer

Hostinger for most people — comparable speed at less than half the renewal price. SiteGround if customer support is your top priority or you’re an agency managing multiple client sites.

Pricing — The Elephant in the Room

Hostinger (Premium) SiteGround (StartUp)
Intro price $1.99/mo $1.99/mo
Renewal price $7.99/mo $17.99/mo
Websites 100 1
Storage 100 GB NVMe 10 GB SSD

Same introductory price, but SiteGround’s renewal is 125% more expensive and gives you 1 website with 10 GB storage, while Hostinger gives 100 websites with 100 GB. Over 3 years at renewal prices, you’d pay $360 more with SiteGround.

item.title: Mailchimp vs Brevo (2026): Contact-Based vs Email-Based Pricing
item.description:
The Quick Answer

Brevo if you have a large contact list and don’t need to email them every day. Mailchimp if you need its massive integration ecosystem or advanced e-commerce analytics.

The pricing model difference is the key: Mailchimp charges you for every contact on your list (even ones you haven’t emailed in months). Brevo charges you for emails actually sent. For most businesses, this means 40-60% lower costs with Brevo.

item.title: Mailchimp vs ConvertKit (2026): All-in-One vs Creator-Focused
item.description:
The Quick Answer

These tools serve fundamentally different audiences. Mailchimp is a general-purpose marketing platform — email, ads, social, landing pages, e-commerce integrations. ConvertKit (now Kit) is laser-focused on creators — writers, podcasters, YouTubers — and lets you sell digital products and subscriptions without needing Shopify or Gumroad.

If you’re a creator monetizing an audience: ConvertKit. If you’re running an e-commerce store or traditional business: Mailchimp.

Pricing Comparison

Feature Mailchimp ConvertKit (Kit)
Free plan 500 contacts, 1,000 sends/mo 10,000 subscribers (limited features)
Paid from $13/mo (500 contacts) $25/mo (1,000 subscribers)
At 5,000 contacts ~$75/mo ~$79/mo
At 10,000 contacts ~$110/mo ~$119/mo
At 50,000 contacts ~$385/mo ~$379/mo

ConvertKit’s free plan is radically more generous — 10,000 subscribers for free vs Mailchimp’s 500. But ConvertKit’s free tier lacks automation and sequences, which limits its usefulness.

item.title: Mailchimp vs MailerLite (2026): Is It Worth Paying 3x More?
item.description:
The Quick Answer

MailerLite wins for most people. It’s half the price, has a cleaner interface, includes a website builder and paid newsletters on all plans, and has significantly higher user satisfaction (G2: 4.7 vs Mailchimp’s 4.3). Mailchimp’s advantage is its massive integration ecosystem (300+ vs 140) and deeper analytics for e-commerce.

Pricing — The Biggest Difference

Contacts Mailchimp (Standard) MailerLite (Growing Business) Savings
500 $20/mo $10/mo 50% less
1,000 $30/mo $15/mo 50% less
5,000 $75/mo $39/mo 48% less
10,000 $110/mo $73/mo 34% less
50,000 $385/mo $289/mo 25% less

MailerLite is consistently 25-50% cheaper at every tier. Over a year at 5,000 contacts, that’s $432 saved — enough to invest in content, ads, or tools.

item.title: ExpressVPN vs Surfshark (2026): Is 3x the Price Worth It?
item.description:
The Quick Answer

Surfshark wins. At $2.19/mo vs ExpressVPN’s $6.67/mo, Surfshark costs roughly one-third the price while offering comparable speeds, better device support (unlimited vs 8), and a built-in ad blocker that ExpressVPN lacks.

ExpressVPN’s only defensible advantages are its superior app design and reliability in China. For everyone else, paying 3x more for ExpressVPN is hard to justify.

Pricing — The Elephant in the Room

Plan ExpressVPN Surfshark Savings
Monthly $12.95 $15.45 ExpressVPN cheaper
1-Year $6.67/mo $2.99/mo Save $44/year with Surfshark
2-Year N/A $2.19/mo Save $107+ with Surfshark

Over 2 years, choosing Surfshark over ExpressVPN saves you over $100. Both offer 30-day money-back guarantees, so you can try risk-free.

item.title: NordVPN vs ExpressVPN (2026): Which VPN Is Actually Better?
item.description:
The Quick Answer

NordVPN wins. It’s nearly half the price of ExpressVPN ($3.39/mo vs $6.67/mo on annual plans), faster in speed tests, has twice as many servers, and includes extras like a built-in ad blocker and malware protection that ExpressVPN doesn’t offer.

ExpressVPN’s only real advantages are: (1) it works more reliably in China, and (2) its app is slightly easier to use. For 99% of people, NordVPN is the better deal.

item.title: NordVPN vs Surfshark (2026): Premium Power vs Budget King
item.description:
The Quick Answer

This is the closest VPN matchup in 2026. Surfshark wins on pure value — it’s $2.19/mo vs NordVPN’s $3.39/mo and offers unlimited device connections. NordVPN wins on raw performance — faster speeds, double the servers, and more advanced features.

If your budget is tight or you have a large family with many devices: Surfshark. If you want the fastest, most feature-rich VPN and don’t mind paying a bit more: NordVPN.
